name: Glaive

api_key.how_to_get: Contact the Glaive team via Telegram or Discord.

delivery.quic: 17-byte auth frame after QUIC handshake: 16-byte UUID as big-endian u128, followed by 1 flags byte

notes: Treat the key as a secret and do not commit it to source control.
Each API key has an individually configured TPS limit.

tip_note: Transactions must include a SOL transfer of at least 0.0001 SOL (100,000 lamports) to one Glaive tip account. Missing or invalid tips are rejected.
cost_on_failure: not explicitly documented; tip is included in the submitted transaction, so it is only transferred if the transaction lands and executes the tip instruction

endpoints.note: Use the region closest to your infrastructure. Mainnet-beta only; no testnet endpoint is documented.

sendTransaction_jsonrpc.description: Standard Solana JSON-RPC sendTransaction format. Drop-in replacement only for sending transactions.

sendTransaction_plain.description: Lightweight HTTP endpoint. Body is the base64-encoded transaction as plain text.

sendTransaction_binary.description: Lowest-overhead HTTP endpoint. Body is raw serialized transaction bytes.

sendTransaction_quic.description: Persistent QUIC connection streaming raw transactions. Intended for latency-sensitive workloads.
sendTransaction_quic.tls: self-signed Solana-style X.509; server certificate verification should be skipped by the client

authentication.stream: Open one unidirectional stream immediately after handshake.
authentication.offset_0_15: API key UUID as big-endian u128, with dashes stripped and hex decoded
authentication.offset_16: flags byte; bit 0 enables MEV protection for every transaction on the connection

submission.stream: Open one unidirectional stream per transaction.

features.leader_fanout: Maintains persistent connections to current and upcoming slot leaders.

mev_protection.description: Skips leaders on Glaive's sandwiching validator blacklist.

http.200: Request processed; check JSON-RPC result or error body
http.400: Bad request on /plain or /binary (e.g. invalid transaction); returns an {error} body

json_rpc.-32601: Method not found; only sendTransaction is supported
json_rpc.-32000: Transaction rejected, including invalid encoding or missing tip

encoding.recommended: base64 for JSON-RPC/plain; raw bytes for binary/QUIC

notes: Glaive does not replace a normal RPC provider for reads, blockhash fetching, simulation, or non-sendTransaction methods.
HTTP examples in the docs use http:// regional endpoints for transaction submission and https:// for /health.
The docs use ?key= for JSON-RPC and ?api-key= for /plain and /binary; preserve this distinction unless confirmed otherwise by Glaive.
